title: Add `go get` install method
body: Probably should also remove the GOPATH instructions, but for now this just adds a `go get`.

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this PR had no activity for 30 days.

What jumped out at me, caused me to use `go get` instead, and caused me to write this PR, was the reference to GOPATH -- it's not likely that newer Go users will even have it set, particularly after 1.17.

I'd be fine with not merging this PR, but then someone ought to tackle getting rid of that apparent dependence on GOPATH and making sure the Makefile still works regardless of how GOPATH is set or if it's not set at all.
I think making the Makefile without GOPATH makes total sense.  If not set, then just set it to $HOME/go, and you get 90% there.

BTW, Does it work without GOPATH?

unset=GOPATH make
CGO_CFLAGS="" CGO_LDFLAGS="-L/usr/lib64 -lgpgme" GO111MODULE=on go build -mod=vendor "-buildmode=pie" -ldflags '-X main.gitCommit=5af5f8a0e7520a93c736a03bf58c26ea006f9ec6 ' -gcflags "" -tags "  " -o bin/skopeo ./cmd/skopeo

Would 
```
git diff
diff --git a/Makefile b/Makefile
index 6bacd12e..94998e2d 100644
--- a/Makefile
+++ b/Makefile
@@ -24,6 +24,7 @@ GO ?= go
 GOBIN := $(shell $(GO) env GOBIN)
 GOOS ?= $(shell go env GOOS)
 GOARCH ?= $(shell go env GOARCH)
+GOPATH ?= $(shell go env GOPATH)
 
 ifeq ($(GOBIN),)
 GOBIN := $(GOPATH)/bin
```
Be enough

Right now checking out into $GOPATH works every time (and, to an extent, steers users who don’t know what $GOPATH is towards distribution packages or containers, though that was not an intentional plot).

It would absolutely make sense to simplify to just say “check it out somewhere and run `make`” _after_ we drop support for those old Go versions (it seems we are testing on Go 1.16, and at least
```
vendor/gopkg.in/yaml.v2/go.mod:go 1.15
```
). And the `Makefile` is already using `-mod=vendor` ~everywhere, commit 8eba94f271ba180a86886dea6e4f8f392f862051 suggests we have needed this since since 1.13 already.

I haven’t now looked how old versions of Go actually allow building Skopeo, and where is the relevant point from which on `GOPATH` is no longer relevant. We might easily be well past that point already, but it does require research and/or testing.

> I think making the Makefile without GOPATH makes total sense. If not set, then just set it to $HOME/go, and you get 90% there.

It can be rather surprising, to the point of suggesting an attack, if `make` in a random checkout starts creating unannounced directories in the home directory.
I think the upstream Makefile can safely assume golang >= 1.15 and be done with this.  If people want to install on older versions then they need to setup their environment correctly.

Thanks for the reminder. I do think that we don‘t want to recommend the `go get` installation mechanism.

WRT the outside-of-GOPATH setups, that’s an interesting discussion but let’s do that in a separate issue/PR, if there is anyone who has the time and interest to pursue it (which won’t be me any time soon, I’m afraid).
